reasoning.typography: System sans (likely Inter/default) with zero character — identical to 10,000 other personal sites. No typographic personality despite 'Writer' claim.
reasoning.hierarchy: Name dominates, list is scannable, paintings provide visual anchor. Works adequately but nothing commanding attention per screen.
reasoning.distinctiveness: Swap 'Sahil' for any tech founder and this works perfectly. Generic chronological essay list + Instagram paintings grid. Zero editorial voice in structure.
reasoning.color: Disciplined: pure black/white with single red 'NEW' badge accent (<5%). Clean restraint, though more default than designed.
reasoning.density: Cautious spacing throughout. Not confident density — just 'safe' uniform padding. Paintings break it up but layout is formulaic breathing room.
reasoning.voice: Chronological reverse blog index is the most obvious IA possible. 'Founder Writer Painter' labels do no work. Structure = default, not story-shaped.

winning_patterns: Restrained color palette with disciplined accent use on 'NEW' badge
Clean readability with proper link underlines and contrast
Paintings at bottom provide human/tactile counterpoint to text
anti_patterns: Generic system font with zero typographic personality for someone calling themselves a 'Writer'
Chronological list requires no editorial thinking — pure CMS default
Could be anyone's personal site — no visual DNA tied to this specific person
Paintings feel tacked on rather than integrated into identity
Essay titles do all the work; layout does none
Identical spacing rhythm = robot-safe rather than confident
